comment.body:
Hi Alma,
Thank you for sharing your work publicly — it’s greatly appreciated.

I have a quick question regarding the pivot levels. 

Today, you noted the upside pivot as 6800.78. Is there a general rule of thumb for how a pivot becomes “active”? For instance, does it require a certain amount of time spent above the level or multiple lower-time-frame candle closes confirming it? Or is it simply considered active as soon as price moves above that level?
Thanks again for your time and insights

comment.body:
Alma, I want to make sure I’m understanding this correctly conceptually. The 3d model on OD with GEX is also the 2D speed model (due to it being the derivative of gamma) right? 

If not let me know and update my ignorance lol

If so, would it be fair to say the MASSIVE positive GEX is what caused the overall net positive speed, but visually it’s slightly negative until the large -GEX pocket?
It was a net positive in the range today but there are spots where it goes negative, where I would think it’s harder to “ladder up” like you said in the weekly? Like the little dips as GEX is increasing to the large wall
